rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers in AGREE contracts by ensuring all referenced features and helpers are visible and properly typed in the same scope.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the actual types of the expressions they represent.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are properly resolved and typed to avoid '<error>' type diagnostics.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annex expressions to prevent 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Validate that AGREE scalar type names (int, real, bool) match the expected types in declarations and expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have consistent types; use `eq` helpers to define intermediate boolean expressions for comparisons.
AGREE `assume` and `guarantee` clauses must reference valid identifiers with known types in the same annex scope.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes unless they are explicitly declared in `with` clauses of the AADL model.
Avoid placing implementation-specific subcomponent ports or paths in component type contracts.
